title: Stelis tepuiensis
taxonomy: Stelis tepuiensis is classified under the kingdom Plantae, with further classification as a member of the family Orchidaceae, which includes orchids. This orchid species is classified under the genus Stelis. The full classification is as follows: Stelis tepuiensis, Orchidaceae, Asparagales, Monocots, Liliopsida, Magnoliophyta, Plantae. This classification highlights the plant's unique position within the plant kingdom. The Stelis tepuiensis classification is subject to ongoing research and potential updates in the field of botany.
uses: Stelis tepuiensis has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Its leaves and stems are reportedly used to treat different ailments, including fever and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that the plant may possess antimicrobial properties, which could contribute to its potential medicinal uses. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Stelis tepuiensis for medicinal purposes. More investigation into its chemical composition and potential health benefits is also required.

intro: Stelis tepuiensis is a species of orchid found in the tepuis of Venezuela, characterized by its unique, epiphytic growth habit and distinctive flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. Research has identified bioactive constituents in Stelis tepuiensis, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are thought to be responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
